mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns do not exceed 55% in months following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based economic strength, driving positive equity returns over a quarter as corporate revenues accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative mean forward return over 60 days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a contraction in the index of industrial production indicates capacity constraints and margin compression, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5-day forward returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when filtered for late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0 percent increases the cost of capital and raises the discount rate for future cash flows, systematically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 65 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent, or the threshold effect shows no statistical edge.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative mean forward returns over 60 days, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rebound in real GDP growth signals renewed business investment and earnings momentum, producing immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rebounds fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns in post-shock recovery periods, or the signal accuracy drops below 50 percent in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and higher corporate profitability, consistently driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.
